item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three source files, update only the allowed modules, then run the test suite against the configured database.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"

item.text: The account model currently maps four fields, while the schema is generated by the repository’s enterprise schema generator. I’ll locate the accounts table definition so the new column is created and migrated safely.

item.text: The schema migration, account create/update mapping, and order-summary projection are in place. I’m running the benchmark tests now.

item.text: The first regression test exposed an existing mismatch: `accounts.created_at` is required by the generated schema, but the account model never supplied it. I’ll preserve the public create API by defaulting that required persistence timestamp to the current time.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'npm test && git diff --check && git diff -- src/schema.mjs src/accounts.mjs src/queries.mjs'
/bin/zsh -lc 'npm test && git diff --check && git diff -- src/schema.mjs src/accounts.mjs src/queries.mjs'

item.text: The next regression failure is also schema-level: the acceptance setup inserts order line items without a `line_item_id`, while the generated schema requires one. I’ll inspect the test’s fixture inserts (read-only) to make the compatibility migration precise rather than guessing at defaults.

The fixture relies on a generated line-item identifier, so I added a database-side default. The prior failed runs left the benchmark’s fixed fixture account in the live test schema; I’ll remove only those known fixture rows before the final clean test run.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "node --input-type=module -e 'import { withDb } from \"./src/db.mjs\"; await withDb(async (client) => { await client.query(\"DELETE FROM invoices WHERE order_id = "'$1", ["ord-ent-1"]); await client.query("DELETE FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id = $1", ["ord-ent-1"]); await client.query("DELETE FROM orders WHERE order_id = $1", ["ord-ent-1"]); await client.query("DELETE FROM accounts WHERE account_id = $1", ["acct-ent-1"]); });'"' && npm test && git diff --check && git status --short"

item.text:
Implemented the account payment preference end to end.

- Added optional enum-backed `preferred_payment_method` to `accounts`.
- Added create, update, and retrieval support in `src/accounts.mjs`.
- Included `preferredPaymentMethod` in `getOrderSummary`.
- Added minimal schema compatibility migrations required by the existing acceptance fixture.

Verified: `npm test` passes.
